body_md:
# Traction & KPIs — Ferrite

**Read for the 24h check:** Deck commercial magnitudes are uncleared and in tension with the only independent distribution counter. Independently verified presence is thin (shipping, launch self-ID users, hackathon)—not cleared MRR, installs, or unit economics. Confidence on scale: **low** until billing/telemetry reconcile; confidence that *some* production residue exists: **medium** on launch-thread users alone.

## Customer / install scale

Deck asserts **3,100 active installations across 240 organizations** [C1]. Public package registry shows **2,900 lifetime downloads** and **~340/week** [C2]—lifetime downloads sit *below* the claimed active-install count. Forum adjudication (agent-derived) converged that the deck install/org figures are uncleared non-evidence until reconciled; they do not carry underwriting [C3].

**Tension (do not smooth):** either distribution is multi-channel (private mirrors, binaries, containers, self-host) and the registry undercounts, or the deck’s “active install” definition is inflated/undefined. No channel map or telemetry definition is on file.

Independent presence (not customer count): GitHub **412 stars, 61 forks, 9 external contributors** [C4]; launch thread had **three commenters who identified themselves as production users** [C5]. Design partners (“Series-B devtools”; “AI lab”) are deck-only, names withheld—unverified partnership substance [C6].

## Revenue / ARR / growth

Deck: **$18k MRR, +40% MoM for 3 months** [C7]. No payment-processor export, billing artifact, plan mix, refunds, or customer-count export on file. ARR is not independently supported; treating $18k×12 as ARR would be invention. Forum (agent-derived): treat MRR/MoM as uncleared non-evidence [C3].

## Unit economics

**CAC, sales cycle, churn, LTV, payback:** not disclosed. No pricing, conversion, or retention series on file.

## Usage metrics

**DAU/WAU/MAU, cache hit rates, CI minutes saved, seats per org:** not disclosed. Closest independent usage proxy is registry download velocity [C2] and self-identified production users on launch [C5]—neither is a DAU series.

## Performance claim (cost)

Deck/application: **10x cost reduction on agent-driven CI** (internal benchmark) [C8]. No protocol, workload mix, or customer before/after spend artifact on file.

## What the independent record does support

Sustained shipping (weekly releases ~20 weeks; founder-dominated commit history) [C9] and Hack-Nation infrastructure-track win [C10] are **execution/validation signals**, not cleared commercial traction. They keep diligence open under registry-order compression; they do not substitute for reconciled paid/active scale.

## Diligence that settles this page

Same-week packet implied by forum instrument (agent-derived): (1) contact launch-thread production users or founder intros for unscripted agent-CI win; (2) trailing-4-month billing export + active-install/org telemetry with distribution-channel map + 10x protocol. Until then, primary magnitudes stay contested or blank.

claims.text: Deck claims 3,100 active installations across 240 organizations.

evidence.quote: Deck page 7: '3,100 active installations across 240 organizations'.

claims.text: Public package registry shows ~340 downloads/week and 2,900 lifetime downloads—lifetime total below the deck’s claimed active-install count.

evidence.quote: Public package registry: 340 downloads/week, 2,900 downloads total lifetime. Tension with deck's '3,100 active installations' — lifetime downloads are below the claimed active-install count.

claims.text: Forum adjudication treats deck 3,100 active installs / 240 orgs and $18k MRR / +40% MoM as uncleared non-evidence that cannot carry underwriting until reconciled.

evidence.quote: Treat deck 3,100 active installs / 240 orgs and $18k MRR / +40% MoM as uncleared non-evidence that cannot carry underwriting until reconciled

claims.text: GitHub repository shows 412 stars, 61 forks, and 9 external contributors.
Launch-thread commenters included three who identified themselves as production users.

evidence.quote: three commenters identified themselves as production users.

claims.text: Deck claims two design partners: one Series-B devtools company and one AI lab (names withheld).

evidence.quote: Two design partners: one Series-B devtools company, one AI lab (names withheld in deck)

claims.text: Deck claims $18k MRR growing 40% month-over-month for 3 months.

evidence.quote: Deck page 9: '$18k MRR, +40% MoM for 3 months'.

claims.text: Deck/application claims 10x cost reduction on agent-driven CI workloads via an internal benchmark.

evidence.quote: 10x cost reduction on agent-driven CI workloads (internal benchmark)

claims.text: Repository shows weekly release cadence for the last 20 weeks and 1,400 commits over 11 months (86% by the founder).

evidence.quote: 1,400 commits over 11 months, 86% by the founder. Weekly release cadence for the last 20 weeks.

claims.text: Ferrite won the infrastructure track at Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon May 2026 in a judged field of ~400 teams.

evidence.quote: Winner, infrastructure track, Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon May 2026 — 'Ferrite: content-addressed caching for agent CI'. Judged field of ~400 teams.

gaps.note: Only deck $18k MRR / +40% MoM (sig-001); no payment-processor or billing export, plan mix, refunds, or customer-count series on file. ARR not independently supported.

No product telemetry or admin export defining 'active install' or org count for the deck window; distribution-channel map (registry vs private mirrors/binaries/containers/self-host) absent—required to reconcile sig-001 vs sig-004.
Not disclosed separately from deck org/install claims; launch-thread yields three self-identified production users only (sig-005), not a paid roster.

gaps.field: Unit economics (CAC, sales cycle, churn, LTV, payback)
gaps.note: Not disclosed; no pricing, conversion, or retention data on file.

gaps.field: Usage metrics (DAU/WAU/MAU, cache hits, CI minutes saved)
gaps.note: Not disclosed; registry downloads and GitHub stars are not product usage series.
Internal-benchmark claim only; no protocol, workload mix, or customer before/after CI spend artifact on file.
Names withheld in deck; no written confirmation of partnership scope/status or founder intros on file.
No structured references; only launch-thread self-ID production users (sig-005) pending contact.
